message.content:
```text
The law of sines is a relationship between the sides and angles of any triangle (not just right triangles). It says that the ratio of the length of a side to the sine of the angle opposite that side is the same for all three sides of the triangle.

Imagine a triangle with angles A, B, C and opposite sides a, b, c respectively:
- side a is opposite angle A
- side b is opposite angle B
- side c is opposite angle C

The law of sines states:

a / sin(A) = b / sin(B) = c / sin(C)

You can also write it in the inverted form:

sin(A) / a = sin(B) / b = sin(C) / c

This law is especially useful for solving triangles when you know:
- two angles and one side (AAS or ASA), or
- two sides and a non-included angle (SSA, with some care about possible ambiguous cases).
```
